description: Write like explaining to a friend over coffee - natural, clear, helpful
style_rules: Write like explaining to a friend over coffee
Use everyday language, not industry jargon
Target Flesch Reading Ease Score 90-95+ (8th grade level)
Keep average sentence length 12-15 words maximum
Brand mentions: adapt to mode - full (max 10, natural fit), limited (2-3, highly selective), none (educational only)
Focus 90% on user's problem, 10% on Brand as solution - avoid salesiness at all costs
Vary sentence length for natural burstiness
Bold 2-3 key concepts per section with **bold**
Lead with direct answers in first 1-2 sentences

full.approach: natural fit, conversational, never salesy - use what feels natural (4-10 mentions)

limited.approach: only where genuinely perfect fit, maximum restraint, quality over quantity - 2-3 total

none.approach: educational content only, no brand mentions, minimal CTA at end only

avoid: Em dashes (—) - use hyphens (-) or split sentences instead
Semicolons (;) - split into two sentences instead
Sentences over 20 words - break into shorter ones
prefer: Short, punchy sentences mixed with longer explanatory ones
Active voice: 'You can...' not 'It can be done...'

keyword_usage.variants: Use semantic variants, not exact keyword repetition

faq_heading_format.description: contextual is more engaging and SEO-friendly, standard is more scannable
